abilities.text: Discard this card to give your next attack roll +2 or to put a weapon card from your discard pile in this card’s place.

cards.flavor: The mechnik was the Tsar’s sword-carrier, a position of great prestige and honor in the court.
cards.raw_source_text: (5) MECHNIK Russian • Special • Title Oruzhenosetz – Action: Discard this card to give your next attack roll +2 or to put a weapon card from your discard pile in this card’s place. The mechnik was the Tsar’s sword-carrier, a position of great prestige and honor in the court. Illustration: John Donahue 4 95/100

cards.name: MITRAY-I

abilities.name: Ijad Moghavemat
abilities.text: After you take damage, roll two dice. If this total is less than your experience, the next attack against you deals –1 damage to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: Afraid of being poisoned, Mithradates took non-lethal doses of poison over many years to develop a resistance to their effects.
cards.raw_source_text: (4) MITRAY-I Persian • Special • Ability Ijad Moghavemat: After you take damage, roll two dice. If this total is less than your experience, the next attack against you deals –1 damage to a minimum of 0. Afraid of being poisoned, Mithradates took non-lethal doses of poison over many years to develop a resistance to their effects. Illustration: Matthew Armstrong 4 70/100

cards.name: OPRICHNIKI

abilities.name: Pihl
abilities.text: Place this card in any unoccupied space in your starting row.

abilities.name: Presledovaniye
abilities.text: Whenever you move your warrior, you may move this card one space (not diagonally). If this card moves into a space occupied by an opposing warrior, you may discard this card to deal 2 damage to that warrior.

cards.flavor: Oscillating between wickedness and repentance, Ivan created the Oprichniki, an order of religious police, to carry out his will, no matter how depraved it might be.
cards.raw_source_text: (3) OPRICHNIKI Russian • Special • Ally Pihl – Reveal: Place this card in any unoccupied space in your starting row. Presledovaniye: Whenever you move your warrior, you may move this card one space (not diagonally). If this card moves into a space occupied by an opposing warrior, you may discard this card to deal 2 damage to that warrior. Oscillating between wickedness and repentance, Ivan created the Oprichniki, an order of religious police, to carry out his will, no matter how depraved it might be. Illustration: William O’Connor 4 85/100

cards.name: PROSVESCHENNIY ABSOLUTISM

abilities.name: Deistvitelnost
abilities.text: Dice and rolls are not affected by card abilities other than attack roll or defense roll bonuses.

abilities.name: Naslediye
abilities.text: Once each match, before dice are rolled, if this card is in your discard pile, prevent the dice or roll from being affected by card abilities other than attack roll or defense roll bonuses.

cards.flavor: Subscribing to Enlightenment belief, Catherine saw her mandate as descending directly from God in order to improve the lives of her people.
cards.raw_source_text: (4) PROSVESCHENNIY ABSOLUTISM Russian • Special • Code Deistvitelnost: Dice and rolls are not affected by card abilities other than attack roll or defense roll bonuses. Naslediye: Once each match, before dice are rolled, if this card is in your discard pile, prevent the dice or roll from being affected by card abilities other than attack roll or defense roll bonuses. Subscribing to Enlightenment belief, Catherine saw her mandate as descending directly from God in order to improve the lives of her people. Illustration: Steven Belledin 4 80/100

cards.name: RHYTON

abilities.name: Meygosari
abilities.text: Once each round, before you are attacked, you may give the attacker the choice to make the attack roll at +0. If they do not, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: “… The fruit bubbled out red juice with white foam. They scooped it up with oxhorns, instead of cups, which had not yet been seen, so that ever after the cup of mixed wine took this divine name of rhyton.” — Dionysiaca, XII 361–362
cards.raw_source_text: (3) RHYTON Persian • Special • Item Meygosari: Once each round, before you are attacked, you may give the attacker the choice to make the attack roll at +0. If they do not, gain 1 life. “… The fruit bubbled out red juice with white foam. They scooped it up with oxhorns, instead of cups, which had not yet been seen, so that ever after the cup of mixed wine took this divine name of rhyton.” — Dionysiaca, XII 361–362 Illustration: Michael Koch 4 75/100

cards.name: RINDI

abilities.name: Prevsoiti Chislennostyu
abilities.text: At the start of each round, choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If this total is less than the chosen warrior’s experience, that warrior’s attacks cannot deal more than base damage this round. Otherwise, your attack rolls against them gain +1 this round.

cards.flavor: The Tsar’s bodyguards were never far from his person.
cards.raw_source_text: (6) RINDI Russian • Special • Ally Prevsoiti Chislennostyu: At the start of each round, choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If this total is less than the chosen warrior’s experience, that warrior’s attacks cannot deal more than base damage this round. Otherwise, your attack rolls against them gain +1 this round. The Tsar’s bodyguards were never far from his person. Illustration: Christopher Appel 4 90/100

cards.name: SHLEM

abilities.name: Uklonitsya
abilities.text: When you are attacked by an attack with a damage bonus or an attack roll bonus greater than +2, you may discard this card. If you do, the attack misses.

cards.flavor: The pointed iron helmet was typically Russian, with the top of the helm varying between conical, conical-spherical, and oniondomed in shape.
cards.raw_source_text: (2) SHLEM Russian • Special • Head Uklonitsya: When you are attacked by an attack with a damage bonus or an attack roll bonus greater than +2, you may discard this card. If you do, the attack misses. The pointed iron helmet was typically Russian, with the top of the helm varying between conical, conical-spherical, and oniondomed in shape. Illustration: James Ryman 4 100/100

cards.name: SIÚLÓIR NA COSACH CROISE

abilities.name: Ag Siúil ar Chos Chroise
abilities.text: Your warrior is not affected by opposing support cards in spaces in the arena.

abilities.name: Abhóg Mór
abilities.text: Once each round, after you roll doubles, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: With the final collapse of Irish resistance at the end of the sixteenth century, these elite stilt-walking troops left with many of their lords to become mercenaries for France and Spain.
cards.raw_source_text: (1) SIÚLÓIR NA COSACH CROISE Irish • Special • Item Ag Siúil ar Chos Chroise: Your warrior is not affected by opposing support cards in spaces in the arena. Abhóg Mór: Once each round, after you roll doubles, you may move one space. With the final collapse of Irish resistance at the end of the sixteenth century, these elite stilt-walking troops left with many of their lords to become mercenaries for France and Spain. Illustration: Raven Mimura 4 20/100

cards.name: �SPW

abilities.name: Tst
abilities.text: Before you make an attack roll, choose “odd” or “even.” If your attack roll matches your choice and the defense roll does not, this and your next attack deal +1 damage. You cannot use this ability again this round.

cards.flavor: One of ancient Egypt’s most mysterious artifacts, the most popular current theory holds that the Sphinx was built by the fourth-dynasty king, Khafre (2558–2532 BC).
cards.raw_source_text: (5) �SPW Egyptian • Special • Location Tst: Before you make an attack roll, choose “odd” or “even.” If your attack roll matches your choice and the defense roll does not, this and your next attack deal +1 damage. You cannot use this ability again this round. One of ancient Egypt’s most mysterious artifacts, the most popular current theory holds that the Sphinx was built by the fourth-dynasty king, Khafre (2558–2532 BC). Illustration: David Ho 4 P24/100

cards.name: TAPU

abilities.name: Whakakore
abilities.text: The next time this game that an opponent hits you with an attack that deals more than its base damage, you may discard this card and one of that opponent’s non-weapon support cards.

cards.flavor: All objects and actions contained tapu, as they are all linked in some way to a god. At certain times, however, the tapu for a given thing or action became so great that failure to give proper respect can have the most dire consequences.
cards.raw_source_text: (5) TAPU Maori • Special • Code Whakakore – Reveal: The next time this game that an opponent hits you with an attack that deals more than its base damage, you may discard this card and one of that opponent’s non-weapon support cards. All objects and actions contained tapu, as they are all linked in some way to a god. At certain times, however, the tapu for a given thing or action became so great that failure to give proper respect can have the most dire consequences. Illustration: Tyler Walpole 4 50/100

cards.name: WAKA

abilities.name: Amatiatia
abilities.text: Once each round, if there is a Water card in play, move to any space in your warrior’s column or row.

cards.flavor: The great, carved canoes of the Maori were used for ocean voyaging as well as carrying raiding parties of warriors into enemy territory.
cards.raw_source_text: (4) WAKA Maori • Special • Cavalry Amatiatia – Action: Once each round, if there is a Water card in play, move to any space in your warrior’s column or row. The great, carved canoes of the Maori were used for ocean voyaging as well as carrying raiding parties of warriors into enemy territory. Illustration: Szucs Grégory 4 40/100
